/

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 28, 1989


Robert K. Stiens
Clinton Reilly Campaign
704 Sansome
San Francisco, CA 94111

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-436

Dear Mr. Stiens:

We are in receipt of your letter of July 20, 1989 (copy
enclosed) seeking written confirmation of the advice I provided to
you in our recent telephone conversation. You have advised me
that you are a volunteer with the Orange County Democratic Central
Committee and have been authorized to seek this advice on behalf
of the committee.

This is to confirm that your letter accurately reflects the
advice I provided to you. If you have any further questions
regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Blanca M. Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:BMB:plh

Enclosure


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

ROBERT K. STIENS
225 S. NEWHOPE #323
SANTA ANA, CA 92704
714/531-9725

Bian Breeze
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J St #800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Breeze,

Thank you for answering my questions regarding the financing of a
political poll by a County Central Committee, and Broad Based
Political Action Committees.

The following is my understanding of our conversation. I would
appreciate your confirmation and clarification as soon as possible.

1. CENTRAL COMMITTEE

A County Central Committee can pay the full cost of a poll from
their overhead account if the results are not shown to any of the
potential candidates.

If the results are shown to someone who is currently a candidate
or has formed an exploratory committee, a portion of the cost of
the poll must be reimbursed to the overhead account form the
Central Committee's Political Action account. Additionally, the
Party must count this as an in-kind contribution to the
candidate, and the candidate and/or exploratory committee must
report the cost as an in-kind contribution. The amount of the in-
kind contribution counts against the $5,000 limit a candidate can
receive per fiscal year from a Broad Based PAC.

If a potential candidate who is shown the poll results becomes a
candidate, or forms an exploratory committee, a portion of the
cost of the poll must be reimbursed to the overhead account form
the Central Committee's Political Action account. Additionally,
the Party must count this as an in-kind contribution to the
candidate, and the candidate and/or exploratory committee must
report the cost as an in-kind contribution. The amount of the in-
kind contribution counts against the $5,000 limit a candidate can
receive per fiscal year from a Broad Based PAC.

2. BROAD BASED POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEE

A Broad Based Political Action Committee can pay the for the full
cost of the poll with the same conditions regarding reporting and
contribution limits for the PAC and candidate/potential
candidate.

Again, thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

Robert K. Stiens

July 25, 1989


Robert K. Stiens
225 S. Newhope #323
Santa Ana, CA  92704

                                                Re:  Letter No. 89-436

Dear Mr. Stiens:

     We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice
under the Political Reform Act on July 24, 1989. Your letter has
been assigned to Bianca Breeze for response. If you have any
questions, you may contact her directly at (916) 322-5901.

     If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. A confirming
response will be released after it has gone through our approval
process. If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you
shortly to advise you. In such cases, the normal analysis, review
and approval process will be followed.

     You should be aware that your letter and our response are
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                                Sincerely,


                                                [illegible]

                                                Kathryn E. Donovan
                                                General Counsel

KED:plh:confadv1

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-89-436

Dear Mr. Stiens:

     We are in receipt of your letter of July 20, 1989 (copy
enclosed) seeking written confirmation of the advice I provided to
you in our recent telephone conversation.  You have advised me
that you are a volunteer with the Orange County Democratic Central
Committee and have been authorized to seek this advice on behalf
of the Committee.

     This is to confirm that your letter accurately reflects the
advice I provided to you.  If you have any further questions
regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        By:  Blanca M. Breeze
                                             Counsel, Legal Division

CENTRAL COMMITTEE A County Central Committee can pay the full cost of a poll from their overhead account if the results are not shown to any of the potential candidates. If the results are shown to someone who is currently a candidate or has formed an exploratory committee, a portion of the cost of the poll must be reimbursed to the overhead account form the Central Committee's Political Action account. The amount of the in- kind contribution counts against the $5,000 limit a candidate can receive per fiscal year from a Broad Based PAC. If a potential candidate who is shown the poll results becomes a candidate, or forms an exploratory committee, a portion of the cost of the poll must be reimbursed to the overhead account form the Central Committee's Political Action account. Additionally, the Party must count this as an in-kind contribution to the candidate, and the candidate and/or exploratory committee must report the cost as an in-kind contribution.
